parts.answer: An explanation that includes two of the following points: • there is a correlation (between vitamin C in the diet and CVD) (1) • effect of vitamin C on an aspect of CVD ACCEPT idea of correlation describe and in either direction. E.g. If vit C is high risk of CVD is low/high e.g. affects plaque formation/LDLs/ cholesterol / damage to endothelium/ atherosclerosis (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark An explanation that includes the following points: • colour change from blue to colourless (1) • because DCPIP is reduced (1) ACCEPT DCPIP gains electrons or gains hydrogen (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark A calculation showing the following steps: • equivalence of DCPIP calculated (1) • concentration of vitamin C calculated and quoted to 1 d.p. (1) • = 1.5 x 10 OR 15 OR ÷26.1 • 15 ÷ 26.1 = 0.6 (mg cm -3 ) Correct answer with no working shown gains both marks (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark A calculation showing the following steps. • correct figures from table manipulated appropriately (1) • percentage calculated and quoted to maximum of 2 d.p (1) • 0.4 ÷ 9.4 • × 100 = 4.26 / 4.3 (%) Correct answer with no working shown gains both marks (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark A graph showing the following features: • axes correctly labelled with units (1) • both standard deviations (SDs) correctly plotted (1) each juice named x 6 and mean concentration of vitamin C (in fruit juice) / mg cm -3 (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark An answer that includes the following. • carton juice has less vitamin C (in all cases) (1) • biggest difference is with lime juice / smallest difference is with orange juice (1) • carton juice content is less variable than fresh / SD of carton juice is smaller than SD of fresh juice (1) • the difference between fresh juice and carton juice is significant / standard deviation of fresh juice and carton juice do not overlap (1) ACCEPT converse ACCEPT converse (3) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark
